title: GripFieldConfig
description: Per-rally configuration of pace-note fields. Each rally defines which fields appear on a pacenote (name, kind, grid placement, render/TTS order, optional inline chip vocabulary). Pace notes then store their values as a JSON object keyed by field.key. Different rallies and disciplines can use entirely different shorthand systems by shipping different field configs. App-specific data (rendering hints unique to one renderer, UX state, etc.) lives under namespaced `extensions` objects on the top-level config, on each field, and on each chip — apps own their own subtree, keyed by a kebab-case app slug.

field.description: A single pace-note field. The combination of key + kind + grid determines where it lives, how it's input, and how it renders.

key.description: Unique field identifier within the rally. Used as the property name under paceNote.fieldValues.

label.description: Display label shown on the canvas slot and pickers.

kind.description: How the field is entered. chips = pick from an inline vocabulary; freetext = type any string; number = numeric input (e.g. distance).

grid.description: Placement on the rally's input grid. Columns are alignment lanes (0-indexed, ordered left-to-right): fields sharing the same colStart align vertically regardless of which row they're on. Lane widths scale to fit content. A span across multiple lanes is expressed with colEnd (inclusive); omit colEnd for a single-lane field. Rows are spatial: row is the vertical position (0-indexed from top) and h is the row span — renderers should preserve these for reliable export.

colStart.description: Leftmost alignment lane the field occupies. 0-indexed.

colEnd.description: Rightmost alignment lane the field occupies (inclusive). Optional; defaults to colStart for a single-lane field. Must be >= colStart (enforced by consumers, not the schema).

h.description: Number of row units the field occupies vertically.

repeatable.description: If true, fieldValues[key] is an array of primitives; otherwise a single primitive.

horizontalRenderOrder.description: Horizontal sequence order when multiple fields share a grid cell (same colStart/row). For example, caution + direction + severity stacked in one slot use horizontalRenderOrder to decide which renders first within the row. Only affects horizontal ordering; vertical placement is driven by row/h. Lower = earlier.

ttsOrder.description: Sequence order in which this field's value(s) are spoken via TTS. Distinct from the 2D rendered layout — TTS is a 1D reading-order traversal, and ttsOrder is how the crew wants those tokens spoken. Lower = earlier.

justify.description: Text alignment of the rendered slot value. Defaults to center.

chips.description: kind:'chips' only — the inline vocabulary for this field. Each chip is one allowed value.

numeric.description: Numeric input parameters. For kind:'number' fields, this defines the scroller/stepper range and is the field's only input mode. For kind:'chips' fields it's an optional augmentation that adds a numeric scroller alongside the chip grid; the value stored in fieldValues can then be either a chip's value (string) or a number, whichever the user picked most recently.

maxLines.description: kind:'freetext' only — soft cap on the number of lines rendered.

extensions.description: App-specific namespaced data attached to this field. Keys are app slugs (kebab-case recommended, e.g. 'grip-note'); values are objects owned by that app. Consumers should pass through unrecognized namespaces unchanged.

chip.description: One entry in a chips-kind field's vocabulary.

value.description: Canonical label stored on pace notes.

audible.description: Optional TTS override if the spoken form differs from the label.

extensions.description: App-specific namespaced data attached to this chip. Keys are app slugs (kebab-case recommended, e.g. 'grip-note'); values are objects owned by that app. For example, a compass-dial renderer can store its `angle` value under its own namespace without other consumers having to know about it.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the field-config schema this document conforms to.

extensions.description: App-specific namespaced data attached to the field-config as a whole (e.g. recce UX flow state, presentation profiles, app-managed metadata). Keys are app slugs (kebab-case recommended, e.g. 'grip-note'); values are objects owned by that app. Consumers should pass through unrecognized namespaces unchanged.
